summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Match Made in Heaven' series with a message titled 'The Fight for Peace.' He opened by reading Romans 12:16, 'live in harmony with one another,' and used a musical analogy to illustrate how different individuals can create beautiful harmony in relationships. Pastor Travis emphasized that our world and relationships often lack peace because we fight each other instead of fighting for peace. He explained that the root of all conflict is the battle of desires within us, as described in James 4:1-2. The real enemy is not other people but spiritual forces of evil, as stated in Ephesians 6:12. Therefore, the key is to 'don't fight the person, fight the problem.' The sermon focused on becoming peacemakers, as Jesus called blessed in Matthew 5:9. Pastor Travis described conflict as being handed a lit 'bomb' of hurtful words, where we have the choice to either detonate it (escalate) or defuse it (make peace). He stressed that the goal in conflict is not to be right but to be righteous. Two biblical keys for being a peacemaker were given: first, ask God to calm your mind, guarding your heart as the source of everything (Proverbs 4:23), and using the shield of faith to extinguish the enemy's flaming arrows (Ephesians 6:16). Second, ask God to guard your mouth, as our words have great power to build up or destroy (James 3:5-10). The message concluded with the assurance from John 16:33 that in Christ we can have peace because He has overcome the world. Pastor Travis invited those who did not know Christ to begin a relationship with Him as the foundation for all other healthy relationships.
mainPoints: The root of all conflict is internal desires, and our real enemy is not other people but spiritual forces; we must fight for peace, not against each other.
In conflict, our goal should be to be righteous, not right, by choosing to be peacemakers who defuse tension rather than escalate it.
Achieving peace requires asking God to guard our minds from destructive thoughts and our mouths from harmful words, relying on His Spirit for self-control.

versesMentioned.text: Live in harmony with one another.

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.

What causes fights and quarrels among you? Don’t they come from your desires that battle within you? You desire but do not have, so you kill. You covet but you cannot get what you want, so you quarrel and fight.
If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
Whoever brings ruin on their family will inherit only wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise.

For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.
If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
In addition to all this, take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
The mind governed by the flesh is death, but the mind governed by the Spirit is life and peace.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Set a guard over my mouth, LORD; keep watch over the door of my lips.

Likewise, the tongue is a small part of the body, but it makes great boasts. Consider what a great forest is set on fire by a small spark. The tongue also is a fire, a world of evil among the parts of the body. It corrupts the whole body, sets the whole course of one’s life on fire, and is itself set on fire by hell. All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles and sea creatures are being tamed and have been tamed by mankind, but no human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison. With the tongue we praise our Lord and Father, and with it we curse human beings, who have been made in God’s likeness. Out of the same mouth come praise and cursing. My brothers and sisters, this should not be.
My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
Fools give full vent to their rage, but the wise bring calm in the end.
A good man brings good things out of the good stored up in his heart, and an evil man brings evil things out of the evil stored up in his heart. For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.
But now you must also rid yourselves of all such things as these: anger, rage, malice, slander, and filthy language from your lips.
Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
"In your anger do not sin": Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry, and do not give the devil a foothold.
I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. In this world you will have trouble. But take heart! I have overcome the world.

Monday.scripture: Romans 12:16 (NIV) - "Live in harmony with one another."
Monday.action: Identify one relationship where tension exists. Make a conscious choice not to see that person as the enemy today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to live in harmony with others today. Show me how to be a peacemaker, starting with my own heart. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: James 4:1-2 (NIV) - "What causes fights and quarrels among you? Don’t they come from your desires that battle within you?"
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a recent conflict. Journal about the internal desire (e.g., to be right, respected, understood) that was at the root of your reaction.
Tuesday.prayer: God, reveal the selfish desires in my heart that lead to conflict. Help me surrender my wants to Your will for peace. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: Ephesians 6:12 (NIV) - "For our struggle is not against flesh and blood..."
Wednesday.action: When you feel irritated or offended by someone today, pause and silently declare: "My struggle is not against this person."
Wednesday.prayer: Heavenly Father, open my spiritual eyes. Help me remember that the real battle is spiritual, and to fight the problem, not the person. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 5:9 (NIV) - "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God."
Thursday.action: In a conversation today, intentionally choose to de-escalate. Use a gentle tone and seek to understand before being understood.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, You are the Prince of Peace. Make me a peacemaker today. Give me wisdom to defuse tension and bring Your calm. Amen.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - "Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it."
Friday.action: Monitor your media intake. If a news story or social media post stirs up anxiety or anger, consciously turn it off or scroll past.
Friday.prayer: Lord, be the guard of my heart and mind. Filter what I allow in, so that only what is good and pleasing to You flows out. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: James 1:19 (NIV) - "Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry."
Saturday.action: Practice the 'listen first' rule in all your conversations today. Count to three before responding to ensure your words are helpful.
Saturday.prayer: God, set a guard over my mouth. May my words today build others up and reflect the love and peace of Christ in me. Amen.
